name: Profile-based Synthetic Responses Are Evaluated with Realism, Diversity, and Utility Checks
statement: Ipci et al. evaluate profile-based synthetic logical-proof responses with diversity and realism evidence, embedding/PCA comparison to authentic responses, and utility for automated grading model training.
quantitative_note: Repository metadata reports N = 585 authentic open-ended logical-proof responses as the comparator dataset.
caveats: The staged extraction is based on the Infoscience metadata and details page rather than prompt-level full text.
Training utility is a downstream quality signal, not proof that synthetic responses can replace real responses.
evaluation_method: Diversity and realism analysis with embedding-based distance metrics and PCA, plus automated-grading model-training utility.
reported_metrics: N = 585 authentic responses; diversity, realism, and training-utility comparisons are summarized on the source page.
limitations: Synthetic-only training is not established as equivalent to real data.
Fine-grained prompt and metric parameters require full-text review.
The finding is bounded to logical-proof response generation and augmentation.

source_locator.section: Infoscience details page, evaluation summary, and results summary
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the source page's diversity, realism, embedding, PCA, and training-utility evaluation description.
